message:
feat(lessons): add lessons for 7 utility tools and fix manifest metadata

Add lesson components for csctf, tru, mdwb, rano, caut, aadc, and
rust_proxy. Wire rano-lesson.tsx (previously orphaned) into index.tsx
and lessons.ts. Add web metadata (display_name, tagline, features,
cli_name, command_example) to all utility tools in the manifest. Add
verified_installer URLs for pt, fsfs, casr, and asb. Close 59 beads
(bd-s9a3t master epic + all downstream integration tasks).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -709,7 +709,25 @@ modules:
         WRAPPER
         chmod +x ~/.local/bin/gemini
       # Apply patches (EBADF crash fix, rate-limit retry 3→1000, quota retry)
-      - curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/misc_coding_agent_tips_and_scripts/main/fix-gemini-cli-ebadf-crash.sh | bash
+      - |
+        security_lib="${ACFS_LIB_DIR:-$HOME/.acfs/scripts/lib}/security.sh"
+        if [[ ! -f "$security_lib" ]]; then
+          echo "agents.gemini: security library not found at $security_lib" >&2
+          exit 1
+        fi
+        if [[ -n "${ACFS_CHECKSUMS_YAML:-}" ]]; then
+          export CHECKSUMS_FILE="$ACFS_CHECKSUMS_YAML"
+        fi
+        # shellcheck disable=SC1090,SC1091
+        source "$security_lib"
+        load_checksums
+        patch_url="${KNOWN_INSTALLERS[gemini_patch]:-}"
+        patch_sha256="$(get_checksum gemini_patch)"
+        if [[ -z "$patch_url" || -z "$patch_sha256" ]]; then
+          echo "agents.gemini: missing verified installer metadata for gemini_patch" >&2
+          exit 1
+        fi
+        verify_checksum "$patch_url" "$patch_sha256" "gemini_patch" | bash
     verify:
       - ~/.local/bin/gemini --version || ~/.local/bin/gemini --help

@@ -0,0 +1,179 @@ +'use client'; + +import { + AlignLeft, + Terminal, + Wand2, + Eye, + Copy, + Settings, + Play, + Shield, +} from 'lucide-react'; +import { + Section, + Paragraph, + CodeBlock, + TipBox, + Highlight, + Divider, + GoalBanner, + CommandList, + FeatureCard, + FeatureGrid, +} from './lesson-components'; + +export function AadcLesson() { + return ( +
+ + Fix malformed ASCII art diagrams that AI agents produce with misaligned boxes and broken lines. + + + {/* Section 1: What Is AADC */} +
} delay={0.1}> + + AADC (ASCII Art Diagram Corrector) automatically + fixes alignment issues, broken box edges, and misconnected lines in ASCII + diagrams. AI models frequently generate diagrams with subtle rendering + errors that AADC corrects. + + + When agents produce architecture diagrams, flowcharts, or tables in ASCII, + the output often has columns that don't align, boxes with gaps, or + connectors that miss their targets. AADC detects and repairs these issues. + + +
+ + } + title="Auto-Repair" + description="Fix alignment and spacing" + gradient="from-blue-500/20 to-indigo-500/20" + /> + } + title="Box Detection" + description="Repair broken box edges" + gradient="from-violet-500/20 to-purple-500/20" + /> + } + title="Preview" + description="See before/after diff" + gradient="from-emerald-500/20 to-teal-500/20" + /> + } + title="Clipboard" + description="Copy fixed output directly" + gradient="from-amber-500/20 to-orange-500/20" + /> + +
+
+ + + + {/* Section 2: Quick Start */} +
} delay={0.15}> + + Fix a diagram from a file or stdin. + + + + + + Use --diff to see exactly what AADC changed before accepting the output. + +
+ + + + {/* Section 3: Commands */} +
} delay={0.2}> + ', description: 'Fix alignment issues in a diagram' }, + { command: 'aadc fix --diff ', description: 'Show changes as a diff' }, + { command: 'aadc check ', description: 'Check for issues without fixing' }, + { command: 'aadc --help', description: 'Show all available options' }, + ]} + /> +
+ + + + {/* Section 4: Example */} +
} delay={0.25}> + + AADC fixes common AI-generated diagram issues. + + + | Server| ++--------+ +-------+ + | + +-------+ + | DB | + +------+ + +# After (fixed): ++----------+ +--------+ +| Client |--->| Server | ++----------+ +--------+ + | + +--------+ + | DB | + +--------+`} + filename="Correction Example" + /> + + + AADC handles box edges, connector lines, padding, and column alignment + in a single pass. + +
+ + + + {/* Section 5: Integration */} +
} delay={0.3}> +
+
+ AADC + Agents +

Post-process agent diagram output

+
+
+ AADC + S2P +

Clean diagrams before including in prompts

+
+
+ AADC + CSCTF +

Fix diagrams in archived conversations

+
+
+ AADC + CM +

Clean architecture diagrams in memory files

+
+
+
+
+ ); +}

@@ -0,0 +1,171 @@ +'use client'; + +import { + BarChart3, + Terminal, + DollarSign, + PieChart, + Clock, + Play, + Shield, +} from 'lucide-react'; +import { + Section, + Paragraph, + CodeBlock, + TipBox, + Highlight, + Divider, + GoalBanner, + CommandList, + FeatureCard, + FeatureGrid, +} from './lesson-components'; + +export function CautLesson() { + return ( +
+ + Track LLM API usage across providers and agents to understand costs and optimize spending. + + + {/* Section 1: What Is CAUT */} +
} delay={0.1}> + + CAUT (Coding Agent Usage Tracker) monitors token + consumption and API costs across all your AI coding agents. It aggregates + usage from Claude, GPT, and Gemini into a single dashboard. + + + When running multiple agents simultaneously, costs can escalate quickly. + CAUT gives you visibility into which agents, sessions, and providers are + consuming the most tokens, helping you optimize your workflow. + + +
+ + } + title="Cost Tracking" + description="Per-provider spend analysis" + gradient="from-blue-500/20 to-indigo-500/20" + /> + } + title="Usage Breakdown" + description="Input vs output tokens" + gradient="from-violet-500/20 to-purple-500/20" + /> + } + title="Time Series" + description="Historical usage trends" + gradient="from-emerald-500/20 to-teal-500/20" + /> + } + title="Multi-Agent" + description="Track across all sessions" + gradient="from-amber-500/20 to-orange-500/20" + /> + +
+
+ + + + {/* Section 2: Quick Start */} +
} delay={0.15}> + + Get a quick overview of your usage. + + + + + + Run caut summary at the end of each day to understand your usage patterns. + +
+ + + + {/* Section 3: Commands */} +
} delay={0.2}> + + + + CAUT reads usage data from agent log files and API response headers. + No additional API calls are made. + +
+ + + + {/* Section 4: Cost Optimization */} +
} delay={0.25}> + + Use CAUT data to optimize your agent spending. + + + +
+ + + + {/* Section 5: Integration */} +
} delay={0.3}> +
+
+ CAUT + RANO +

Network traffic feeds usage tracking data

+
+
+ CAUT + CAAM +

Track usage per account for billing insights

+
+
+ CAUT + NTM +

See cost per agent in multi-agent swarms

+
+
+ CAUT + TRU +

Measure TRU compression savings in real tokens

+
+
+
+
+ ); +}

@@ -0,0 +1,172 @@ +'use client'; + +import { + FileText, + Terminal, + Zap, + Download, + Globe, + Shield, + Settings, + Play, +} from 'lucide-react'; +import { + Section, + Paragraph, + CodeBlock, + TipBox, + Highlight, + Divider, + GoalBanner, + CommandList, + FeatureCard, + FeatureGrid, +} from './lesson-components'; + +export function CsctfLesson() { + return ( +
+ + Convert AI chat share links into clean Markdown and HTML files for archiving and reference. + + + {/* Section 1: What Is CSCTF */} +
} delay={0.1}> + + CSCTF (Chat Shared Conversation to File) downloads + conversations from ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Grok share links, converting + them into clean Markdown and HTML files you can keep forever. + + + AI providers can remove shared conversations at any time. CSCTF preserves + your best conversations locally with proper formatting, code blocks, and + metadata intact. + + +
+ + } + title="Multi-Provider" + description="ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok" + gradient="from-blue-500/20 to-indigo-500/20" + /> + } + title="Dual Output" + description="Markdown + HTML per conversation" + gradient="from-violet-500/20 to-purple-500/20" + /> + } + title="Offline Archive" + description="Keep conversations forever" + gradient="from-emerald-500/20 to-teal-500/20" + /> + } + title="Code Preserved" + description="Syntax highlighting intact" + gradient="from-amber-500/20 to-orange-500/20" + /> + +
+
+ + + + {/* Section 2: Quick Start */} +
} delay={0.15}> + + Pass a share link and CSCTF handles the rest. + + + + + + CSCTF auto-detects the provider from the URL. No flags needed. + +
+ + + + {/* Section 3: Commands */} +
} delay={0.2}> + "', description: 'Convert a single conversation' }, + { command: 'csctf -o ~/archive ""', description: 'Save to specific directory' }, + { command: 'csctf --md-only ""', description: 'Output Markdown only' }, + { command: 'csctf --html-only ""', description: 'Output HTML only' }, + ]} + /> + + + The generated Markdown works great as input for other tools like S2P or + as context for new AI conversations. + +
+ + + + {/* Section 4: Batch Processing */} +
} delay={0.25}> + + Archive multiple conversations at once by passing a file of URLs. + + + urls.txt << 'EOF' +https://chatgpt.com/share/abc123 +https://claude.ai/share/xyz789 +https://gemini.google.com/share/def456 +EOF + +# Process all URLs +csctf --batch urls.txt -o ~/ai-archive/`} + filename="Batch Mode" + /> +
+ + + + {/* Section 5: Integration */} +
} delay={0.3}> + + CSCTF fits naturally into the agent workflow. + + +
+
+ CSCTF + CASS +

Archive conversations, then search them with CASS

+
+
+ CSCTF + S2P +

Convert archived chats into LLM-ready prompts

+
+
+ CSCTF + CM +

Extract patterns from past conversations into memory

+
+
+ CSCTF + Beads +

Link archived conversations to task issues

+
+
+
+
+ ); +}

@@ -0,0 +1,175 @@ +'use client'; + +import { + Globe, + Terminal, + Zap, + FileText, + Code, + Settings, + Play, + Shield, +} from 'lucide-react'; +import { + Section, + Paragraph, + CodeBlock, + TipBox, + Highlight, + Divider, + GoalBanner, + CommandList, + FeatureCard, + FeatureGrid, +} from './lesson-components'; + +export function MdwbLesson() { + return ( +
+ + Convert web pages into clean Markdown that AI agents can read and reason about. + + + {/* Section 1: What Is MDWB */} +
} delay={0.1}> + + MDWB (Markdown Web Browser) fetches web pages and + converts them into clean Markdown, stripping out ads, navigation, and + scripts. The result is perfect for feeding into LLMs as context. + + + When AI agents need information from the web, raw HTML is noisy and + token-expensive. MDWB extracts just the content, preserving headings, + code blocks, lists, and links in a format LLMs handle efficiently. + + +
+ + } + title="Any Web Page" + description="Works with most websites" + gradient="from-blue-500/20 to-indigo-500/20" + /> + } + title="Clean Output" + description="No ads, scripts, or clutter" + gradient="from-violet-500/20 to-purple-500/20" + /> + } + title="Code Blocks" + description="Preserves code formatting" + gradient="from-emerald-500/20 to-teal-500/20" + /> + } + title="Fast" + description="Built in Rust for speed" + gradient="from-amber-500/20 to-orange-500/20" + /> + +
+
+ + + + {/* Section 2: Quick Start */} +
} delay={0.15}> + + Convert any URL to Markdown with a single command. + + + guide.md + +# Pipe directly to an AI agent +mdwb "https://docs.example.com/api" | claude "summarize this API"`} + filename="Basic Usage" + /> + + + Pipe MDWB output directly into AI agents for instant web research. + +
+ + + + {/* Section 3: Commands */} +
} delay={0.2}> + "', description: 'Convert a URL to Markdown' }, + { command: 'mdwb -o output.md ""', description: 'Save output to a file' }, + { command: 'mdwb --links ""', description: 'Include link URLs in output' }, + { command: 'mdwb --help', description: 'Show all available options' }, + ]} + /> + + + MDWB handles JavaScript-rendered pages, documentation sites, and + blog posts. It works best with content-focused pages. + +
+ + + + {/* Section 4: Use Cases */} +
} delay={0.25}> + + MDWB shines when you need web content in LLM-friendly format. + + + tokio-docs.md + +# Get error context from StackOverflow +mdwb "https://stackoverflow.com/questions/12345" > context.md + +# Archive a blog post for reference +mdwb "https://blog.example.com/architecture-decisions" > arch.md + +# Feed multiple pages to an agent +for url in $(cat urls.txt); do + mdwb "$url" +done | claude "analyze these documents"`} + filename="Use Cases" + /> +
+ + + + {/* Section 5: Integration */} +
} delay={0.3}> + + MDWB connects web knowledge to the agent workflow. + + +
+
+ MDWB + TRU +

Fetch docs, then compress for maximum context

+
+
+ MDWB + FSFS +

Index downloaded pages for local semantic search

+
+
+ MDWB + CSCTF +

Archive both web pages and AI conversations

+
+
+ MDWB + Brenner +

Feed research papers into Brenner corpus

+
+
+
+
+ ); +}

@@ -0,0 +1,198 @@ +'use client'; + +import { + Network, + Terminal, + Eye, + BarChart3, + Bug, + Filter, + Settings, + Shield, +} from 'lucide-react'; +import { + Section, + Paragraph, + CodeBlock, + TipBox, + Highlight, + Divider, + GoalBanner, + CommandList, + FeatureCard, + FeatureGrid, +} from './lesson-components'; + +export function RanoLesson() { + return ( +
+ + Monitor and debug AI CLI network traffic to understand what your agents send and receive. + + + {/* Section 1: What Is RANO */} +
} delay={0.1}> + + RANO is a network observer purpose-built for AI coding CLIs. + It intercepts HTTP requests between your local agents (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI) + and their APIs, logging requests and responses for analysis. + + + When agents behave unexpectedly, RANO helps you see exactly what's being sent + and received, making it invaluable for debugging prompt issues, token usage tracking, + and understanding agent behavior. + + +
+ + } + title="Transparent Proxy" + description="Zero-config interception" + gradient="from-blue-500/20 to-indigo-500/20" + /> + } + title="Usage Stats" + description="Token and cost tracking" + gradient="from-violet-500/20 to-purple-500/20" + /> + } + title="Smart Filtering" + description="Provider-aware log parsing" + gradient="from-emerald-500/20 to-teal-500/20" + /> + } + title="Debug Mode" + description="Full request/response logging" + gradient="from-amber-500/20 to-orange-500/20" + /> + +
+
+ + + + {/* Section 2: Quick Start */} +
} delay={0.15}> + + Start observing AI CLI traffic. + + + + + + RANO automatically detects which AI CLIs are installed and configures interception + for each one. + +
+ + + + {/* Section 3: Analyzing Traffic */} +
} delay={0.2}> + + RANO provides structured views of captured API traffic. + + + + + + Token counts help you understand which agents are consuming the most context + and optimize your prompting strategy accordingly. + +
+ + + + {/* Section 4: Debug Mode */} +
} delay={0.25}> + + When an agent behaves unexpectedly, debug mode captures everything. + + + + + + Debug mode logs full request and response bodies, which may include sensitive data. + Use it for troubleshooting, then stop it when done. + +
+ + + + {/* Section 5: Integration */} +
} delay={0.3}> +
+
+ RANO + CAUT +

Detailed traffic feeds into usage tracking

+
+
+ RANO + CAAM +

See which account each request uses

+
+
+ RANO + NTM +

Monitor traffic across all spawned agents

+
+
+ RANO + Beads +

Track API issues as beads for follow-up

+
+
+
+ + + + {/* Section 6: Best Practices */} +
} delay={0.35}> + + Run RANO during development to build intuition about what your agents are actually + sending. You'll often discover wasted tokens or redundant API calls. + + + + RANO is a debugging tool, not a permanent fixture. Running it continuously adds + latency and disk usage. Enable it when investigating issues, disable it otherwise. + +
+
+ ); +}

@@ -0,0 +1,173 @@ +'use client'; + +import { + Shield, + Terminal, + Zap, + Activity, + Lock, + Settings, + Play, + Eye, +} from 'lucide-react'; +import { + Section, + Paragraph, + CodeBlock, + TipBox, + Highlight, + Divider, + GoalBanner, + CommandList, + FeatureCard, + FeatureGrid, +} from './lesson-components'; + +export function RustProxyLesson() { + return ( +
+ + Route and inspect network traffic through a transparent proxy for debugging API issues. + + + {/* Section 1: What Is Rust Proxy */} +
} delay={0.1}> + + Rust Proxy is a high-performance transparent proxy + for inspecting HTTP/HTTPS traffic between your tools and external services. + It sits between clients and servers, logging requests and responses without + modifying them. + + + When debugging network issues with AI APIs or other services, Rust Proxy + lets you see exactly what's going over the wire without changing your + application configuration. + + +
+ + } + title="Transparent" + description="No app changes needed" + gradient="from-blue-500/20 to-indigo-500/20" + /> + } + title="HTTPS Support" + description="TLS interception capable" + gradient="from-violet-500/20 to-purple-500/20" + /> + } + title="Low Latency" + description="Minimal overhead in Rust" + gradient="from-emerald-500/20 to-teal-500/20" + /> + } + title="Full Logging" + description="Headers, bodies, timing" + gradient="from-amber-500/20 to-orange-500/20" + /> + +
+
+ + + + {/* Section 2: Quick Start */} +
} delay={0.15}> + + Start the proxy and route traffic through it. + + + + + + Set HTTPS_PROXY environment variable to route any tool's + traffic through the proxy. + +
+ + + + {/* Section 3: Commands */} +
} delay={0.2}> + +
+ + + + {/* Section 4: Use Cases */} +
} delay={0.25}> + + Common situations where Rust Proxy helps. + + + /dev/null +rust_proxy logs --last 1 --headers + +# Measure API latency +rust_proxy logs --timing + +# Filter by domain +rust_proxy logs --domain api.anthropic.com`} + filename="Debug Scenarios" + /> + + + Rust Proxy can log sensitive data including API keys in headers. + Use it only for debugging and stop it when done. + +
+ + + + {/* Section 5: Integration */} +
} delay={0.3}> +
+
+ RP + RANO +

Low-level proxy complements RANO's AI-specific observer

+
+
+ RP + CAUT +

Traffic data feeds usage tracking

+
+
+ RP + CAAM +

Verify which API keys are being used

+
+
+ RP + Beads +

Track network issues as beads

+
+
+
+
+ ); +}

@@ -0,0 +1,182 @@ +'use client'; + +import { + Minimize2, + Terminal, + Zap, + FileCode, + BarChart3, + Settings, + Play, + Shield, +} from 'lucide-react'; +import { + Section, + Paragraph, + CodeBlock, + TipBox, + Highlight, + Divider, + GoalBanner, + CommandList, + FeatureCard, + FeatureGrid, +} from './lesson-components'; + +export function TruLesson() { + return ( +
+ + Compress source code into token-optimized notation so more context fits in each LLM request. + + + {/* Section 1: What Is TRU */} +
} delay={0.1}> + + TRU (toon_rust) converts source code into a compact, + token-optimized notation that preserves semantic meaning while dramatically + reducing token count. Feed more code context into LLMs without hitting limits. + + + When working with large codebases, context windows fill up fast. TRU + compresses code by 40-70% in token count while keeping it understandable + to LLMs, letting you include more files in each request. + + +
+ + } + title="40-70% Smaller" + description="Dramatic token reduction" + gradient="from-blue-500/20 to-indigo-500/20" + /> + } + title="Multi-Language" + description="Rust, Python, TypeScript, Go" + gradient="from-violet-500/20 to-purple-500/20" + /> + } + title="Token Counting" + description="Before/after comparisons" + gradient="from-emerald-500/20 to-teal-500/20" + /> + } + title="Rust Speed" + description="Processes large repos instantly" + gradient="from-amber-500/20 to-orange-500/20" + /> + +
+
+ + + + {/* Section 2: Quick Start */} +
} delay={0.15}> + + Convert a file or directory to token-optimized notation. + + + + + + Use --stats to see exactly how many tokens you saved. + +
+ + + + {/* Section 3: Commands */} +
} delay={0.2}> + ', description: 'Compress a file to token-optimized format' }, + { command: 'tru compress --stats ', description: 'Compress with token count comparison' }, + { command: 'tru decompress ', description: 'Restore from compressed notation' }, + { command: 'tru --help', description: 'Show all available options' }, + ]} + /> +
+ + + + {/* Section 4: How It Works */} +
} delay={0.25}> + + TRU applies language-aware transformations that LLMs can still understand. + + + u64 { + if n <= 1 { + return n; + } + let mut a: u64 = 0; + let mut b: u64 = 1; + for _ in 2..=n { + let temp = a + b; + a = b; + b = temp; + } + b +} + +# TRU compressed (lower token count): +fn fib(n:u64)->u64{if n<=1{ret n} +let(mut a,mut b)=(0u64,1u64); +for _ in 2..=n{let t=a+b;a=b;b=t}b}`} + filename="Compression Example" + /> + + + The compressed output is still valid, readable code. LLMs understand it + perfectly since they process tokens, not visual formatting. + +
+ + + + {/* Section 5: Integration */} +
} delay={0.3}> + + Combine TRU with other flywheel tools for maximum context efficiency. + + +
+
+ TRU + S2P +

Generate prompts, then compress for more context

+
+
+ TRU + FSFS +

Search results compressed for LLM consumption

+
+
+ TRU + PCR +

Post-compaction reminders with compressed context

+
+
+ TRU + CASR +

Cross-agent session context stays within limits

+
+
+
+
+ ); +}
